meta.description: Default developmental skill baseline for the Amoeba Learner Profile. Domain names match the app exactly. Critical Thinking skills distributed into Mathematical Thinking, Scientific & Environmental Inquiry, and Language & Communication. Collaboration & Relational Skills built out as a full domain. Sources: CCSS (Math, ELA), NGSS, CDC, CASEL, EYFS, ISTE.

sources.CCSS-Math: Common Core State Standards for Mathematics
sources.CCSS-ELA: Common Core State Standards for English Language Arts
sources.CASEL: Collaborative for Academic, Social, and Emotional Learning Framework
sources.ISTE: International Society for Technology in Education Standards

domain_notes.Collaboration & Relational Skills: Covers empathy, teamwork, and interpersonal integrity. Absorbs skills previously in Critical Thinking & Problem-Solving that relate to collaborative reasoning, perspective-taking in group contexts, and shared problem-solving.
domain_notes.Mathematical Thinking: Absorbs logical reasoning, systematic problem-solving, and mathematical argumentation skills from the former Critical Thinking domain.
domain_notes.Scientific & Environmental Inquiry: Absorbs inquiry process skills (hypothesis, evidence evaluation, claims) from the former Critical Thinking domain.
domain_notes.Language & Communication: Absorbs argumentation, critical reading, and source evaluation skills from the former Critical Thinking domain.

skills.name: Recognize letters and their sounds
skills.description: Identify all uppercase and lowercase letters. Associate each letter with its primary sound (phonemic awareness).

skills.name: Speak clearly in complete sentences
skills.description: Express ideas in complete sentences with appropriate volume and eye contact. Take turns in conversation.

skills.name: Listen and respond to stories and instructions
skills.description: Listen attentively to read-alouds and short instructions. Retell key events or follow two-step directions.

skills.name: Write letters and simple words
skills.description: Write own name and familiar words. Represent ideas through drawing combined with emergent writing.

skills.name: Understand print concepts
skills.description: Understand that print carries meaning, how to hold a book, directionality (left to right, top to bottom), and that words are separated by spaces.

skills.name: Decode and read simple texts fluently
skills.description: Apply phonics knowledge to decode unfamiliar words. Read grade-level texts with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.

skills.name: Comprehend and retell narrative texts
skills.description: Identify key details, characters, setting, and problem/solution in stories. Retell using beginning, middle, and end.

skills.name: Write sentences with correct mechanics
skills.description: Write complete sentences with capitalization and end punctuation. Use spaces between words consistently.

skills.name: Ask and answer questions about a text
skills.description: Ask and answer questions (who, what, where, when, why, how) about key details in a text. Use evidence from the text.

skills.name: Distinguish facts from opinions
skills.description: Identify statements as factual claims or opinions. Understand that facts can be verified and opinions reflect perspectives.

skills.name: Read and understand informational texts
skills.description: Identify main idea and supporting details in nonfiction. Use text features (headings, captions, glossary).

skills.name: Write structured paragraphs with a clear purpose
skills.description: Write opinion, informative, and narrative paragraphs with a topic sentence, supporting details, and a conclusion.

skills.name: Use context clues to determine word meaning
skills.description: Use context and word structure (prefixes, suffixes, roots) to determine the meaning of unfamiliar words.

skills.name: Compare and contrast multiple texts on a topic
skills.description: Explain the similarities and differences between two texts on the same topic. Identify how different authors treat the same information.

skills.name: Deliver a short prepared presentation
skills.description: Plan and deliver a short oral presentation on a chosen topic. Use appropriate pacing, volume, and visual support.

skills.name: Construct and critique written arguments
skills.description: Build a written argument with a clear claim and supporting evidence. Identify weaknesses in own and others' arguments.

skills.name: Write multi-paragraph essays with a thesis
skills.description: Write well-structured essays with an introduction, body paragraphs, and conclusion. State and develop a clear claim.

skills.name: Cite evidence from texts to support analysis
skills.description: Quote or paraphrase text evidence to support interpretation or argument. Distinguish evidence from opinion.

skills.name: Identify author's purpose and point of view
skills.description: Analyze how an author's purpose, perspective, or bias influences the content and structure of a text.

skills.name: Conduct research and synthesize information
skills.description: Gather information from multiple sources, evaluate source credibility, and synthesize findings in a written product.

skills.name: Engage in formal academic discussion and debate
skills.description: Participate in structured discussions and formal debate. Present arguments with evidence, respond to counterarguments respectfully.

skills.name: Analyze complex literary texts
skills.description: Analyze theme, character development, figurative language, and structure in complex literary works. Interpret meaning beyond the literal level.

skills.name: Write persuasive and argumentative texts
skills.description: Construct written arguments with a clear claim, logical reasoning, relevant evidence, and counterargument acknowledgment.

skills.name: Evaluate media and digital information critically
skills.description: Assess the credibility, bias, and purpose of information in digital and media formats. Identify misinformation strategies. Recognize cognitive biases and logical fallacies.

skills.name: Adapt communication style to audience and purpose
skills.description: Adjust vocabulary, tone, and format to suit different audiences and contexts — formal writing, peer conversation, public presentation.

skills.name: Count and represent numbers to 20
skills.description: Count forward and backward to 20, represent quantities using objects, drawings, or numerals.

skills.name: Compare quantities (more, fewer, equal)
skills.description: Identify and describe which group has more, fewer, or the same number of objects up to 10.

skills.name: Understand basic shapes and spatial relationships
skills.description: Identify and describe 2D shapes (circle, square, triangle, rectangle). Understand positional language: above, below, beside, inside, outside.

skills.name: Sort, classify, and recognize patterns
skills.description: Sort objects by one attribute (color, size, shape). Identify, copy, and extend repeating patterns (AB, ABB) using objects or movement.

skills.name: Engage in cause-and-effect thinking
skills.description: Understand that actions have consequences. Predict what will happen if a variable changes in a simple situation. Try different approaches when one does not work.

skills.name: Add and subtract within 20
skills.description: Use objects, drawings, and equations to add and subtract within 20. Understand the relationship between addition and subtraction.

skills.name: Understand place value to 100
skills.description: Understand that two-digit numbers represent tens and ones. Compare two-digit numbers using <, =, >.

skills.name: Measure length using standard units
skills.description: Measure and compare lengths of objects using centimeters and inches. Understand that measurement requires a consistent unit.

skills.name: Interpret simple graphs and data
skills.description: Read and interpret picture graphs and bar graphs. Answer questions using data (how many more, how many total).

skills.name: Break a problem into steps and check work
skills.description: Identify the component steps of a math problem. Execute steps in sequence. Check work at each stage and identify where an error occurred.

skills.name: Multiply and divide within 100
skills.description: Know multiplication facts for single-digit numbers. Understand the relationship between multiplication and division.

skills.name: Understand fractions as parts of a whole
skills.description: Represent fractions (halves, thirds, quarters) on a number line and in real-world contexts. Compare simple fractions.

skills.name: Calculate area and perimeter
skills.description: Find the area and perimeter of rectangles and irregular shapes. Understand area as covering square units.

skills.name: Represent and interpret data in tables and graphs
skills.description: Collect, organize, and display data in frequency tables, bar graphs, and line plots. Draw conclusions from data.

skills.name: Use multiple strategies to solve problems and justify choices
skills.description: Recognize that there are multiple valid approaches to a problem. Choose and justify a strategy. Compare strategies with peers and evaluate which is most efficient.

skills.name: Operate with decimals and fractions
skills.description: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide fractions and decimals. Convert between forms. Apply in real-world problems.

skills.name: Understand ratios and proportional relationships
skills.description: Understand ratio concepts, use ratio reasoning to solve problems. Recognize proportional relationships in tables and graphs.

skills.name: Analyze statistical data with measures of center
skills.description: Calculate mean, median, mode, and range. Understand what these measures tell you about a dataset and identify their limitations.

skills.name: Identify relevant information and evaluate a solution
skills.description: Distinguish relevant from irrelevant information in a problem. Identify what is missing. Assess whether a solution actually addresses the problem and consider improvements.

skills.name: Use algebraic expressions and equations
skills.description: Write and evaluate expressions with variables. Solve one-step and two-step equations and inequalities.

skills.name: Understand proportional relationships and percentages
skills.description: Solve multi-step ratio, rate, and percent problems including markup, discount, tax, and simple interest.

skills.name: Apply geometric reasoning
skills.description: Understand properties of angles, triangles, and polygons. Apply formulas for area, surface area, and volume. Use coordinate geometry.

skills.name: Understand functions and linear relationships
skills.description: Define and graph functions. Understand slope and y-intercept. Model real-world situations with linear equations.

skills.name: Apply probability and statistical reasoning
skills.description: Understand probability as likelihood. Calculate theoretical and experimental probability. Interpret data distributions and communicate complex reasoning clearly.

skills.name: Observe and describe the natural world
skills.description: Use the five senses to observe and describe properties of objects and natural phenomena. Ask questions about what is noticed.

skills.name: Sort and classify natural objects
skills.description: Group animals, plants, rocks, or materials by observable properties. Describe the rule for grouping.

skills.name: Understand basic needs of living things
skills.description: Identify that plants and animals need food, water, air, and space to survive. Recognize humans as living things with the same needs.

skills.name: Observe weather and seasonal patterns
skills.description: Observe and record daily weather. Identify patterns across seasons (temperature, precipitation, daylight). Connect patterns to the natural environment.

skills.name: Ask testable questions and make predictions
skills.description: Identify a question that can be answered through observation or simple testing. Make a prediction (hypothesis) and explain the reasoning behind it.

skills.name: Conduct simple investigations and record results
skills.description: Plan and conduct a simple experiment. Record observations using drawings, tally marks, or basic data tables. Identify what the results show.

skills.name: Understand properties of materials and matter
skills.description: Describe and compare properties of materials (hardness, flexibility, transparency, texture). Identify changes to materials.

skills.name: Understand plant and animal life cycles
skills.description: Describe the stages of life cycles for common plants and animals. Understand that offspring resemble parents.

skills.name: Understand ecosystems and food webs
skills.description: Describe how organisms in an ecosystem depend on each other and their environment. Construct a simple food chain and food web.

skills.name: Understand forces and motion
skills.description: Describe how forces (push, pull, gravity, friction) affect the motion of objects. Plan investigations to test the effect of forces.

skills.name: Understand Earth's systems and natural resources
skills.description: Describe how wind, water, and ice shape Earth's surface. Understand renewable and non-renewable resources and their responsible use.

skills.name: Use data to support or refute a claim
skills.description: Analyze data from an investigation to determine whether it supports or contradicts a hypothesis. Identify limitations of the data and what further investigation is needed.

skills.name: Understand the structure and function of living systems
skills.description: Explain how cells, organs, and body systems work together. Connect structure to function in plants and animals.

skills.name: Understand energy transfer and transformation
skills.description: Describe how energy is transferred between objects and transformed from one form to another (heat, light, sound, electrical, kinetic).

skills.name: Understand Earth's place in the solar system
skills.description: Explain the relative positions and motions of Earth, the Moon, and the Sun. Connect to patterns of day, night, tides, and seasons.

skills.name: Apply the engineering design process
skills.description: Define a real problem, brainstorm solutions, prototype, test, and iterate. Evaluate the solution against defined criteria and constraints.

skills.name: Apply systematic inquiry to answer a question
skills.description: Formulate a question, identify and evaluate evidence, draw a reasoned conclusion, and identify what would change the conclusion. Document the full process.

skills.name: Understand chemical reactions and atomic structure
skills.description: Describe how atoms combine to form molecules. Understand the basics of chemical reactions: reactants, products, conservation of mass.

skills.name: Understand genetics and heredity
skills.description: Explain how traits are passed from parents to offspring. Understand the role of genes, chromosomes, and environmental influences on expression.

skills.name: Understand human impact on Earth's systems
skills.description: Analyze how human activity affects Earth's systems. Evaluate evidence for climate change, biodiversity loss, and resource depletion. Propose and assess mitigation strategies.

skills.name: Design and communicate an independent scientific investigation
skills.description: Design a complete investigation with a clear question, method, controlled variables, data collection, analysis, and conclusion. Communicate findings to an audience and respond to questions.

skills.name: Explore materials and tools for making
skills.description: Experiment with art materials (paint, clay, collage, crayons) and tools. Describe choices made in creating.

skills.name: Express ideas and stories through drawing
skills.description: Create drawings that communicate a story, idea, or experience. Begin to include recognizable representations of people, objects, and environments.

skills.name: Engage in imaginative and dramatic play
skills.description: Participate in imaginative play, taking on roles and using objects symbolically. Develop and sustain a narrative during play.

skills.name: Respond to music, movement, and visual art
skills.description: Describe a response to music or artwork using feeling words. Move in response to rhythm and tempo.

skills.name: Create a complete artwork with intent
skills.description: Plan and complete an artwork or creative product with a stated intention. Explain what choices were made and why.

skills.name: Write and tell original stories
skills.description: Compose original stories with a recognizable beginning, middle, and end. Include characters and a simple problem and solution.

skills.name: Perform for a small audience
skills.description: Present a short performance (song, poem, dramatic play, dance) to a small audience. Show awareness of the audience and respond to feedback.

skills.name: Use elements of art in composition
skills.description: Intentionally use line, color, shape, and space to create expressive compositions. Describe how these elements contribute to the work.

skills.name: Develop a creative project over multiple sessions
skills.description: Sustain focus on a creative project across multiple sessions. Revise and refine based on self-assessment and feedback.

skills.name: Use narrative structure and voice in writing
skills.description: Write narratives that establish a situation, narrator, and sequence of events. Use descriptive language and dialogue.

skills.name: Analyze and interpret creative works
skills.description: Describe and interpret artwork, music, or performance using specific vocabulary. Connect the work to its context and intended meaning.

skills.name: Experiment with digital and mixed media
skills.description: Use digital tools (photography, video, audio, design software) as expressive media. Combine digital and physical materials intentionally.

skills.name: Develop a distinct creative voice or style
skills.description: Identify recurring themes, preferences, or techniques in their own work. Make intentional stylistic choices that reflect personal perspective.

skills.name: Connect creative work to cultural and historical context
skills.description: Explain how artworks and creative traditions reflect the culture and time in which they were made. Compare across cultures.

skills.name: Use creative process to explore complex ideas
skills.description: Use creative output (writing, art, design, performance) as a mode of inquiry. Explore ambiguous or complex themes through making.

skills.name: Create and present a substantial independent creative work
skills.description: Produce a significant self-directed creative work (portfolio, performance, project, written piece). Present and defend creative choices to an audience.

skills.name: Give and receive constructive critique
skills.description: Provide specific, evidence-based feedback on peers' creative work. Receive critique with openness and use it to improve.

skills.name: Design and prototype a solution to a real problem
skills.description: Apply design thinking: empathize with users, define the problem, ideate, prototype, and test. Deliver a real or functional solution.

skills.name: Identify and name basic emotions
skills.description: Recognize and name common emotions (happy, sad, angry, scared, surprised) in self and others. Use feeling words in conversation.

skills.name: Follow routines and manage transitions
skills.description: Participate in group settings by following predictable routines. Move between activities without significant distress.

skills.name: Manage frustration and seek help appropriately
skills.description: Identify when feeling frustrated or overwhelmed and use simple coping strategies (take a breath, ask for help). Begin to self-regulate.

skills.name: Show care and basic empathy toward others
skills.description: Notice when a peer is upset and offer simple help or comfort. Begin to show empathy through words or actions.

skills.name: Understand own strengths and areas for growth
skills.description: Identify things they are good at and things they find hard. Show willingness to try challenging tasks without giving up.

skills.name: Show responsibility for personal tasks
skills.description: Complete assigned responsibilities without constant reminders. Take ownership of mistakes and attempt to fix them.

skills.name: Recognize emotions and personal triggers
skills.description: Identify what situations tend to trigger strong emotions. Notice physical signals of emotional escalation and apply a calming strategy.

skills.name: Demonstrate persistence through difficulty
skills.description: Stay engaged with a difficult task without giving up. Use strategies to cope with frustration. Distinguish between effort and fixed ability.

skills.name: Set and work toward a personal goal
skills.description: Identify a meaningful goal, break it into steps, track progress, and reflect on outcomes.

skills.name: Show ethical reasoning in decisions
skills.description: Identify ethical dimensions of a decision. Consider impact on self and others. Reflect on personal values as a guide to action.

skills.name: Understand and manage complex emotions
skills.description: Identify mixed or complex emotions (e.g., proud and embarrassed simultaneously). Use a range of coping strategies. Reflect on emotional patterns over time.

skills.name: Understand mental health and emotional wellbeing
skills.description: Describe what mental health means. Identify signs of stress, anxiety, and burnout. Know strategies for maintaining wellbeing and when to ask for support.

skills.name: Develop a growth mindset toward learning
skills.description: Understand that abilities grow through effort and practice. Reframe setbacks as information rather than failure. Seek feedback actively.

skills.name: Reflect on personal identity and values
skills.description: Articulate personal values, interests, and strengths. Understand how identity is shaped by experience, culture, and relationships.

skills.name: Manage stress and seek support when needed
skills.description: Recognize signs of stress or overwhelm. Use a range of coping strategies. Reach out to trusted adults or peers appropriately and without shame.

skills.name: Demonstrate self-directed learning
skills.description: Identify a topic or skill of genuine interest and pursue it independently. Manage time, evaluate progress, and adapt strategies without external prompting.

skills.name: Demonstrate gross motor coordination
skills.description: Run, jump, hop, skip, and climb with developing coordination and balance. Participate in active play.

skills.name: Demonstrate fine motor skills for daily tasks
skills.description: Hold pencils and scissors correctly. Use hands to manipulate small objects, buttons, and fasteners with increasing control.

skills.name: Understand basic hygiene and self-care
skills.description: Wash hands at appropriate times. Understand basic dental hygiene and personal care routines. Manage own basic needs independently.

skills.name: Understand basic food groups and healthy eating
skills.description: Identify foods that support health and energy. Understand that a variety of foods is part of a healthy diet.

skills.name: Participate in structured physical activity
skills.description: Engage with focus and effort in structured games, sports, or movement activities. Follow rules and safety guidelines.

skills.name: Demonstrate body awareness and spatial control
skills.description: Move safely in shared spaces, adjust speed and direction in response to others, and demonstrate awareness of personal space.

skills.name: Understand the connection between movement and wellbeing
skills.description: Describe how physical activity affects mood, energy, and sleep. Identify activities they enjoy for their own health and wellbeing.

skills.name: Develop a physical skill through deliberate practice
skills.description: Set a physical challenge (catching, balancing, a sport skill) and practice it consistently over time. Track and reflect on improvement.

skills.name: Understand components of physical fitness
skills.description: Identify and describe strength, flexibility, endurance, and coordination. Connect these components to long-term health outcomes.

skills.name: Make healthy choices in food, sleep, and activity
skills.description: Explain the role of sleep, nutrition, and physical activity in maintaining health. Make deliberate healthy choices in everyday contexts.

skills.name: Understand puberty and body changes
skills.description: Understand the physical and emotional changes associated with puberty. Know that these are normal developmental processes and vary between individuals.

skills.name: Develop a personal fitness routine
skills.description: Design and maintain a basic personal fitness plan with varied activity types. Reflect on progress and adjust the plan over time.

skills.name: Understand online safety and digital wellbeing
skills.description: Identify safe and risky online behaviors. Understand screen time effects on sleep and mood. Know how to respond to online conflict or unwanted contact.

skills.name: Understand risk, consent, and personal boundaries
skills.description: Articulate concepts of personal boundaries and consent in physical and social contexts. Recognize unsafe situations and know how to respond.

skills.name: Manage personal health and self-care independently
skills.description: Take initiative for personal health practices including sleep, nutrition, hygiene, and physical activity without adult prompting.

skills.name: Understand substance use and health consequences
skills.description: Describe the physical and social consequences of substance use. Identify strategies for making safe decisions under social pressure.

skills.name: Take turns and share with others
skills.description: Share materials, take turns in games and activities, and wait without significant distress. Acknowledge others' right to participate.

skills.name: Play cooperatively with peers
skills.description: Join group play, contribute to shared goals, and adjust own behavior to fit the group's needs. Begin to negotiate roles.

skills.name: Listen actively to others
skills.description: Give full attention when others speak. Avoid interrupting. Ask questions to understand rather than to respond.

skills.name: Resolve simple conflicts with peers
skills.description: Identify a conflict, listen to another's perspective, and attempt a fair resolution. Know when to ask an adult for help.

skills.name: Build and maintain friendships
skills.description: Initiate and sustain positive peer relationships. Show awareness of what makes a good friend. Repair relationships after conflict.

skills.name: Contribute fairly in group tasks
skills.description: Accept and fulfill a role within a group. Complete the assigned contribution without relying on others to cover it. Acknowledge others' contributions.

skills.name: Show empathy and perspective-taking
skills.description: Consider another person's feelings and viewpoint before responding. Adjust behavior based on awareness of how actions affect others.

skills.name: Navigate peer pressure and make independent decisions
skills.description: Recognize peer pressure situations. Use assertive communication to express own views while respecting others. Make decisions that align with personal values.

skills.name: Negotiate and reach shared agreements
skills.description: Identify areas of disagreement within a group, propose compromises, and work toward a solution that acknowledges multiple perspectives.

skills.name: Give and receive feedback constructively
skills.description: Offer specific, respectful feedback on others' work or ideas. Receive feedback without defensiveness and use it to improve.

skills.name: Manage group dynamics and shared accountability
skills.description: Monitor whether the group is on track. Address unequal participation respectfully. Hold self and others accountable without blame.

skills.name: Analyze problems from multiple perspectives before deciding
skills.description: Understand that complex problems involve multiple stakeholders with different interests. Deliberately seek out perspectives different from your own before forming a conclusion.

skills.name: Lead and support others in a group
skills.description: Take initiative when needed, support others' participation, navigate disagreement constructively, and keep a group focused on a shared goal.

skills.name: Collaborate across difference
skills.description: Work effectively with people who have different backgrounds, communication styles, or opinions. Recognize how difference strengthens group output.

skills.name: Demonstrate interpersonal integrity
skills.description: Keep commitments made to others. Speak honestly even when it is uncomfortable. Acknowledge mistakes to the group and take corrective action.

skills.name: Navigate conflict constructively in complex situations
skills.description: Approach serious conflicts with curiosity rather than defensiveness. Use negotiation and mediation strategies. Know when disengagement is the right choice.

skills.name: Co-create a substantial shared product
skills.description: Plan, produce, and deliver a significant collaborative output (project, performance, publication). Divide work equitably, integrate contributions, and present jointly.

skills.name: Give feedback that improves group reasoning
skills.description: Identify weaknesses in a group's shared reasoning or plan. Raise concerns clearly and propose improvements. Distinguish between critiquing ideas and critiquing people.

skills.name: Build and sustain trust in relationships
skills.description: Understand what builds and erodes trust. Consistently act in ways that are reliable, honest, and respectful over time. Repair trust after it has been broken.

skills.name: Recognize that people have different backgrounds and traditions
skills.description: Notice and describe differences in how families celebrate, eat, dress, and speak. Show curiosity rather than judgment.

skills.name: Identify own cultural identity and family practices
skills.description: Describe family traditions, foods, languages, and practices that are meaningful. Connect these to a sense of personal identity.

skills.name: Show respect for people who are different
skills.description: Use respectful language and behavior toward people with different abilities, backgrounds, or appearances. Speak up when someone is treated unkindly.

skills.name: Understand basic community roles and responsibilities
skills.description: Identify community helpers and their roles. Understand that communities depend on people contributing to shared needs.

skills.name: Understand that the world has many languages and cultures
skills.description: Identify that different countries have different languages, foods, and customs. Show curiosity about cultures beyond their own.

skills.name: Understand basic human rights and fairness
skills.description: Describe what it means for something to be fair or unfair. Identify basic rights that all people should have. Discuss real situations where these are not honored.

skills.name: Understand how geography shapes culture and daily life
skills.description: Explain how climate, location, and resources influence how people live, work, and build communities in different places.

skills.name: Understand historical events and their causes
skills.description: Explain how past events led to present-day conditions. Identify multiple causes and effects of key historical events.

skills.name: Communicate across cultural differences
skills.description: Adapt communication style with awareness of cultural norms. Ask questions to understand rather than assuming. Recognize when a misunderstanding may be cultural.

skills.name: Engage with civic processes and democratic participation
skills.description: Understand how rules and laws are made in democratic systems. Participate in class or community decision-making processes. Understand the concept of civic responsibility.

skills.name: Analyze bias and representation in media and texts
skills.description: Identify whose perspectives are included and excluded in a text, image, or media product. Analyze how this shapes understanding of events or groups.

skills.name: Understand global interdependence
skills.description: Explain how economies, environments, and political systems are interconnected globally. Identify how local actions can have global effects.

skills.name: Investigate a social issue and propose action
skills.description: Research a local or global social issue, evaluate multiple perspectives, and propose an informed course of action or advocacy.

skills.name: Understand systems of power, privilege, and inequality
skills.description: Describe how systems of power have historically distributed advantage and disadvantage. Connect historical patterns to present-day inequalities.

skills.name: Engage across cultural and ideological difference
skills.description: Engage respectfully and curiously with people who hold significantly different cultural, political, or ideological views. Distinguish disagreement from disrespect.

skills.name: Take informed civic action
skills.description: Identify a real community or global need, evaluate options for civic engagement, and take meaningful action (petition, service, advocacy, creative work).

skills.name: Reflect on own cultural assumptions and biases
skills.description: Identify ways that their own cultural background shapes their assumptions and worldview. Demonstrate willingness to revise assumptions when presented with new information.
